heading: Why trust this Orlando neighborhood guide
intro: This guide is built from real knowledge of how Orlando's different areas work for tourists — what each zone is actually like to stay in and navigate, not just what the hotel marketing says.

items.title: Covers the theme park logistics honestly
items.text: Getting between Disney, Universal, and I-Drive is the central logistics question in Orlando. This guide explains exactly what each area means for your daily transport — and what it costs.

items.title: On-site vs off-site assessed fairly
items.text: On-site Disney and Universal hotels are worth it in specific situations — and not worth the premium in others. This guide tells you when, not just what the hotels want you to hear.

items.title: Budget options covered
items.text: From $60/night vacation rentals in Kissimmee to $600/night Disney Deluxe resorts — the guide covers what you actually get at each level and when it makes sense.

items.title: Current for 2026
items.text: Disney's early entry perks and Universal's Express system change. This guide reflects current policies, not outdated information from previous years.

footer: If you want an honest guide to where to stay in Orlando — with the real trade-offs — this was built to help you choose right.
